*&
F etn P oltrtcnr- P RACTICEs C ortauISSIoN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 I Fax (916) 322-0886
September 9, 2008
Benjamin P. de MaYo
County Counsel
County of Orange
P.O. Box 1379
Santa Ana, California 927 02- L37 9
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-125
This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance regarding the
mass mailing provisions oi the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r_ Since your q_uestion is
general in niture, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.' Please
irote that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may have
already taken place.
QUESTION
May a colulty supervisor send a self-mailer that meets the definition of "mass
nrailing" *t i.h inclirdes his or her name in the letterhead, in the returx address, and in the
destination address of the detachable self-mailer porlion of the mass mailing?
CONCLUSION
A self-mailer that includes the name of an elected officer only in the letterhead'
the return adilress, and in the destination address of the detachable self-mailer portion of
the mailing is permissible under Regulation 18901(bX 1) and staff's interpretations of that
provision..
I The political Retbrm Act is cgntained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
p.litical practices Commission are conrained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the Calitornia
Clode Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Califirrnia Code of
Reculations, unless otherwise indicated.
2 Intirrmal assistance dges not provicle the requestor with the inrmunity provided by an opinion or
tormal written advice. (Section 83 I l'11 rcgulation l8-129(cX3)' copy enclosedl'


File No. I-08-125
Page No. 2
FACTS
A county supervisor wishes to send a mailing that meets the definition of "mass
mailing." The supervisor would like to include his or her name in letterhead, the retum
address, and the destination address of the detachable self-mailer portion of the mass
mailing. You submitted a sample mailer sent by another elected officer to illustrate the
format of the proposed mailer, but did not provide a copy of the mailer the supervisor
wishes to send.
However, you described the proposed mailer as consisting of one piece of paper
folded into three parts. One part will be a letter from the county supervisor in which the
only mention of the supervisor's name will be in the agency's letterhead. The second
part contains the address of the intended recipient and a return address containing the
supervisor's name. This is essentially the envelope portion of the mailer. The last part is
a tear-off postcard preaddressed to the supervisor on which the recipient can reqllest
additional information.
ANALYSIS
Section 89001 states that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense." Regulation 18901(a) states that "except as provided in subdivision (b),
a mailing is prohibited by section 89001 if the following criteria are met:
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at
his or her residence, place of employment or business, or post
office box...
"(2) The item sent either:
"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or
"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to
an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer.
"(3XA) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public
moneys; or
"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00
are paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.


File No. I-08-125
Page No. 3
"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in
a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited.reqllest and any item described in subdivision (b)."
ln order for an item to be a prohibited "mass mailing" as defined under the Act,
all four enumerated criteria listed above must be met. (See Regulation 18901(aX1) -
(aX4).) If all four criteria under subdivision (a) apply, the mass mailing shall be
prohibited unless an exception under subdivision (b) of Regulation 18901 applies. You
have acknowledged that the mailer in questions meets the four criteria.
Exceptions
Regulation 18901(b) provides a variety of exceptions to the mass mailing
prohibition. Generally, each use of an elected officer's name, office, and any
other reference to an elected officer must satisfy the requirements of one of the
exceptions in the regulation.
1.. Use of the Officer's Name in the Letterhead and Return Addresses of
Envelopes.
ln pertinent part, Regulation 18901(bXl) provides:
"Any item in which the elected officer's name appears only in the
letterhead or logotype of the stationery, forms (including 'For Your
Information' or 'Compliments of cards), and envelopes of the
agency sending the mailing, or of a committee of the agency, or of
the elected officer, or in a roster listing containing the names of all
elected officers of the agency. [n any such item, the names of all
elected officers must appear in the same type size, typeface, type
color, and location. Such item may not include the elected
officer's photograph, signature, or any other reference to the
elected officer, except as specifically permitted in this subdivision
(bX1) or elsewhere in this regulation."
Thus, the use of the elected officer's name in the letterhead of his or her agency's
stationery, forms, and envelopes is permitted. On that basis, the Commission staff has
applied this exception to use of the official's name in the return address on the agency's
envelopes (Kowagoe Advice Letter, No. 4,-96-097) and in the retum address on the back
of the document that serves as the envelope for the mailing. (Calhoun Advice Letter, No.
A-92-043.) The regulation does not expressly permit use of the official's name on a card
provided to a mail recipient to request additional information. However, the Commission
staff has by advice extended the exception to this use of the official's name (See Calhoun
Advice Letter, supra.)


File No. I-08-125
Page No.4
As described in your letter, your question concerns a mailer consisting of one
piece of paper folded into three parts. One part will be a letter from the county supervisor
in which the only mention of the supervisor's name will be in the agency's letterhead.
The second part contains the address of the intended recipient and a return address
containing the supervisor's name. This is essentially the envelope portion of the mailer.
These two parts of the mailer clearly fall within the exception in regulation 18901(bX1)
and are permitted. The last part is a tear-off postcard preaddressed to the supervisor on
which the recipient can request addition information. As discussed above, the exception
in Regulation 18901(b)(l) does not expressly apply to this type of mail piece. However,
because the staff has by advice letter extended the exception to apply to this, we will
continue to permit it under the regulation. Consequently, each of the uses of the
supervisor's name would fall under the exceptions discussed above.
2. Other references.
You have not provided a copy of the mailer you intend to send. Please note that
prior advice letters have concluded that personal pronouns such as "I," "my," "me," and
"we" were references to the elected officer "affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing," and that the use of the word "office" was a personal reference to the
elected officer. In addition, references to the office of the officer may also be prohibited.
You may want to write us again when you have developed the mailer you wish to send.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact
me ar (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
W. Wallace
istant General Counsel
Division
Jwwjgl
Enclosure

Section 89001 states that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at

a mailing is prohibited by section 89001 if the following criteria are met:
"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at
his or her residence, place of employment or business, or post
office box...

"(3XA) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public
moneys; or
"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00
are paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.
"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in
a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited.reqllest and any item described in subdivision (b)."
ln order for an item to be a prohibited "mass mailing" as defined under the Act,

Regulation 18901(b) provides a variety of exceptions to the mass mailing
prohibition. Generally, each use of an elected officer's name, office, and any

agency sending the mailing, or of a committee of the agency, or of

elected officers must appear in the same type size, typeface, type
color, and location. Such item may not include the elected
officer's photograph, signature, or any other reference to the

provided to a mail recipient to request additional information. However, the Commission

As described in your letter, your question concerns a mailer consisting of one

because the staff has by advice letter extended the exception to apply to this, we will

2. Other references.
You have not provided a copy of the mailer you intend to send. Please note that
